content.text: When user selects a Component Bend Local test (e.g., L1 or L2) from the Rules and Action list, system shall open a right-side configuration drawer.
the configuration drawer to open when I select a Component Bend Local test
I can configure its evaluation parameters.
I am viewing the Rules and Action list,
I select a Component Bend Local test (e.g., L1 or L2),
the system opens a right-side configuration drawer.

2.title: Display Test Name and ID in Header

content.text: System shall display the test name and test ID in the drawer header (e.g., Component Bend (L1) — Configuration).
the test name and ID displayed in the drawer header
I can identify the selected test configuration.
the system shows the test name and ID (e.g., Component Bend (L1) — Configuration).
System shall display the test scope badge (e.g., Local) in the header.
I know the scope classification of the test.
the configuration drawer header is visible,
the system shows the scope badge (e.g., Local).
System shall provide a close (X) icon to dismiss the configuration drawer.
a close (X) icon in the configuration drawer header
I can quickly dismiss the drawer.
the system provides a close (X) icon.
When user clicks close icon, system shall close the drawer without saving unsaved changes.
the drawer to close without saving when I click the close icon
the configuration drawer contains unsaved changes,
the system closes the drawer without saving the changes.
System shall display descriptive subtitle explaining the test logic (e.g., Geometric deformation tolerance).
a descriptive subtitle explaining the test logic
I understand the purpose of the configuration.
the system shows a subtitle explaining the test logic (e.g., Geometric deformation tolerance).
System shall group configuration sections by linked element types (e.g., Beam, Upright, Bracing).
configuration sections grouped by element types
I can easily configure rules for each element.
the system groups them by linked element types such as Beam, Upright, and Bracing.
System shall allow expand/collapse behaviour for each element section.
each element section to support expand and collapse behaviour
I can manage screen space while reviewing configurations.
I click an expand or collapse control,
the system expands or collapses the selected section.
System shall display Beam section with Lateral Deformation Rule description.
I can review the lateral deformation rule.
the system displays the Beam section with the Lateral Deformation Rule description.
System shall display “Divisor” input mandatory field in Beam section.
a Divisor input field in the Beam section
I can configure allowable deformation parameters.
the system displays a mandatory Divisor input field.
System shall display “Allowable lateral deformation” formula (Span / 400).
I understand how allowable deformation is calculated.
the Beam configuration section is visible,
the system shows the formula Allowable Lateral Deformation = Span / 400.
I understand the critical deformation limit.
the system shows the Red threshold formula 2 × (Span / 400).
System shall automatically derive Span (L) from Digital Twin data. and display text “Span L is automatically derived from the Digital Twin. No manual configuration required.”
the span value automatically derived from Digital Twin data
Digital Twin geometry data is available,
the system automatically derives Span (L) and displays the message “Span L is automatically derived from the Digital Twin. No manual configuration required.”  indicating it is auto-derived.
System shall restrict manual editing of auto-derived Span value.
the auto-derived span value to be non-editable
the span value is derived automatically,
the value is displayed in the configuration drawer,
the system restricts manual editing of the span field.
System shall display Upright configuration inputs: Cross-Aisle Limit (mm) and Down-Aisle Limit (mm).
the Upright configuration section is visible,
the system shows fields for Cross-Aisle Limit (mm) and Down-Aisle Limit (mm).
System shall mark mandatory fields with an asterisk (*).
I know which inputs are required.
the system marks them with an asterisk (*).
System shall validate numeric input for limit fields (mm values only).
the limit input fields are editable,
the system validates that only numeric millimetre values are accepted.
System shall prevent saving if mandatory fields are empty or invalid.
the system to block saving when mandatory fields are invalid
mandatory fields are empty or invalid,
the system prevents saving and displays validation errors.
System shall display Bracing configuration section with Base Limit (mm) input.
I can configure base deformation limits.
the system displays the Base Limit (mm) input field.
System shall provide Cancel button to discard changes.
the system discards the changes and closes the drawer.
System shall provide Save Configuration button to persist changes.
the system saves the configuration settings.
When user clicks Save Configuration, system shall validate inputs before saving.
the system to validate inputs before saving
the system validates the inputs before saving the configuration.
System shall display configuration status indicator (e.g., Not yet configured).
I know whether the test configuration has been completed.
the test rule is displayed in the Rules and Actions list,
the system displays a status indicator (e.g., Not yet configured).
